title: Quick Pressure-Cooker Dal Tadka

subs.name: masoor dal (red lentil) — cooks even faster
subs.notes: only 2 whistles needed; colour turns orange-gold

subs.name: omit for a no-onion-no-garlic version
subs.notes: increase asafoetida to 2 pinches to compensate

subs.name: oil for a vegan version
subs.notes: the flavour is lighter but still good

steps.text: Rinse toor dal three times until the water runs mostly clear. In the pressure cooker, combine the dal with tomato, onion, green chilli, turmeric, 2 cups water, and a generous pinch of salt.
Pressure cook on high heat until 3 whistles, then lower heat and cook 2 more minutes. Turn off and let pressure release naturally for 8 minutes before opening.
Open the cooker and mash the dal lightly with the back of a ladle — you want a creamy consistency, not a paste. Add more hot water if it is too thick. Taste and adjust salt.

Heat ghee in a small tadka pan or ladle on medium-high until shimmering. Add cumin seeds and let them darken for 5 seconds. Add garlic slices and fry until golden and beginning to crisp. Add dried red chillies and asafoetida, then red chilli powder — stand back, it will spit.
Pour the sizzling tadka immediately over the dal and cover for 30 seconds so the aroma infuses. Stir through, finish with coriander leaves, and serve hot over steamed rice or with roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: toor-dal → masoor dal (red lentil) — cooks even faster (only 2 whistles needed; colour turns orange-gold); ghee → oil for a vegan version (the flavour is lighter but still good); garlic → omit for a no-onion-no-garlic version (increase asafoetida to 2 pinches to compensate)
